data.title: Socket Mobile expands DuraScan series for logistics, manufacturing
data.description:
• Socket Mobile (NASDAQ:SCKT) has announced the expansion of its DuraScan product family to offer new options and scanning capabilities tailored to the logistics and manufacturing sectors.
  • The DuraScan product family is known for its rugged durability, advanced data capture functionalities, and versatile applications across multiple industries.
  • Socket Mobile is expanding the series with the new DuraScan D762, a high-performance 1D/2D barcode scanner designed to meet the specific requirements of warehousing, logistics, and manufacturing environments.
  • "At Socket Mobile, we are committed to empowering our logistics and manufacturing partners with the tools they need to operate at peak performance," said Dave Holmes, Chief Business Officer at Socket Mobile.
  • This launch marks just the beginning as the Co. continue developing additional solutions to support the evolving demands of logistics and manufacturing operations.

data.title: Potential DOD budget cuts give Palantir 'more opportunity,' not less: Wedbush
data.description:
If the Department of Defense goes through with the type of budget cuts suggested by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's leaked memo, it would give Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) “more opportunity,” not less, Wedbush Securities argued.

Shares of Palantir tumbled 12% in morning trading on Thursday, following yesterday's 10% decline. In the two sessions, the stock has experienced a bear-market correction.

“The bears, which have hated Palantir from $12 to $120 in the last 18 months, now have found their latest 'silver bullet' negative thesis around PLTR being exposed to these budget cuts, and we saw a sharp sell-off in trading accordingly once this news about [the Department of Defense] hit in the afternoon,” Wedbush analysts led by Dan Ives wrote in a note to clients.

“This is exactly the opposite [of] how we believe these DOD cuts will play out as in our view Palantir's unique software approach will enable the company to gain more IT budget dollars at the Pentagon....not less, despite these initial knee-jerk reactions from the Street,” the analysts continued. “Palantir is so well positioned for this new disciplined spending environment at the Pentagon, and this will ultimately be a positive growth catalyst as the various programs are scrutinized and as Karp & Co. get a bigger seat at the table in the Beltway.”

Hegseth's memo, obtained by the Washington Post, called for 8% annual spending cuts over the next five years. However, 17 categories were said to be exempted from the cuts, including security at the southern U.S. border, the modernization of the U.S. nuclear weapon arsenal and missile defense, one-way attack drones and other areas.

Delving deeper, Wedbush analysts said that the “stepped-up” investments in artificial intelligence coming from the Trump administration, namely Project Stargate, should benefit Palantir. It is in the “the sweet spot” to benefit from federal government AI-related spending, including getting FedRAMP approval for its entire cloud services, the analysts explained.

“Palantir remains one of our top names to own in 2025, and we believe this sell-off represents another opportunity with PLTR generating traction across both federal and commercial for its entire portfolio,” the analysts, who maintained their Outperform rating and $120 price target, added.

Separately, on Thursday, Palantir was initiated with a Buy rating and $141 price target at Loop Capital.

(This story has been updated to include additional information on the stock decline in the second paragraph.)

data.title: SELLAS Life Sciences Group reports positive results from mid-stage lymphoma treatment trial
data.description:
SELLAS Life Sciences Group (NASDAQ:SLS) announced on Thursday promising mid-stage trial results for SLS009, a highly selective CDK9 inhibitor, in patients with relapsed or refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL).

Stock up 3.2% in morning trade.

The study showed an overall response rate (ORR) of 67%, more than double the expected response rate of zanubrutinib alone. Among the responders, one patient achieved a complete response, while three experienced partial responses with lesion shrinkage of 89%, 78%, and 56%, the company said.

As of the last follow-up, six out of nine patients were still alive. Additionally, the adverse events observed were consistent with those expected from zanubrutinib alone.

data.title: UL Solutions targets mid-single-digit organic growth for 2025 with 24% adjusted EBITDA margin
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: UL Solutions Inc. (NYSE:ULS) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Jenny Scanlon highlighted UL Solutions' strong performance in its inaugural year as a public company, achieving $2.9 billion in revenue for 2024, up 7.2% year-over-year. Organic growth was 8.7%, driven by strength across all segments and geographies. The industrial segment led with 9.4% growth, followed by the consumer segment at 5.6%, and software and advisory at 5.0%.

  • Scanlon noted key drivers, including the global energy transition, electrification of everything, and digitalization. The company completed two acquisitions in 2024: BatterieIngenieure for battery testing and TesTneT for hydrogen testing. It also expanded its battery testing facilities in Michigan and announced a new automotive and battery testing center in Korea.

  • CFO Ryan Robinson reported Q4 2024 consolidated revenue of $739 million, an 8.0% increase year-over-year with 9.5% organic growth. Adjusted EBITDA rose 27.1% to $169 million, and adjusted EBITDA margin expanded by 350 basis points to 22.9%. Adjusted net income was $102 million, up from $62 million in Q4 2023.

  • Robinson attributed strong industrial segment performance to 13.9% organic growth, driven by certification testing and services tied to energy and automation. Consumer segment revenue grew 6.5% organically, supported by demand in retail and consumer technology. Software and advisory saw 5.2% organic growth, bolstered by software solutions for compliance and sustainability.

Outlook

  • For 2025, UL Solutions projects mid-single-digit organic revenue growth and an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 24%, up from 22.9% in Q4 2024. The growth will be supported by operating leverage, industrial segment outperformance, and a continued focus on productivity.

  • Capital expenditures are expected to be 7% to 8% of revenue in 2025, slightly down from 2024 but aligned with investments in new labs and software to meet demand. The effective tax rate is anticipated to rise to 26%, impacted by OECD Pillar 2 provisions and the absence of a 2024 tax reserve benefit.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 2024 was $0.49 compared to $0.29 in Q4 2023. Consolidated revenue of $739 million compared to analysts' estimate of $726.5 million. Organic growth was driven by industrial segment strength and ongoing certification services.

  • Free cash flow for 2024 was $287 million, up from $252 million in 2023, despite higher capital expenditures of $237 million. The company's balance sheet remains strong with $298 million in cash and an investment-grade credit rating.

  • Industrial segment adjusted EBITDA margin was 32%, up 510 basis points year-over-year. Consumer and software margins also improved, driven by revenue growth and cost management.

Q&A

  • Stephanie Yee, JPMorgan: Asked about 2025 outlook by segment. CFO Ryan Robinson emphasized that all segments are expected to contribute to mid-single-digit consolidated growth, with industrial benefiting from megatrends like energy transition and data center demand.

  • George Tong, Goldman Sachs: Inquired whether double-digit industrial growth could continue. Robinson noted strong tailwinds but highlighted tougher year-over-year comparisons in 2025.

  • Tom Ross, William Blair: Asked about structural margin expansion beyond 2025. Robinson stated the company sees opportunities for continued growth and margin improvement across all segments.

  • Shlomo Rosenbaum, Stifel: Queried demand trends for battery labs. Scanlon highlighted growth in energy storage systems, industrial-scale batteries, and vehicle electrification as key drivers.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on sustainability of industrial growth and margin improvement. Questions about tariffs, certification services, and battery lab demand reflected a mix of interest in long-term drivers and short-term risks.

  • Management displayed confidence, attributing strong results to operational leverage and strategic investments. Phrases like "we believe" and "we are confident" underscored their positive outlook.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language for 2025 showed a shift to moderate growth expectations following 8.7% organic growth in 2024. Management focused on sustaining profitability and cash flow while navigating higher tax rates and tougher comparisons.

  • Analysts focused on industrial growth sustainability and margin expansion, consistent with prior discussions but with added scrutiny on certification services normalization and FX headwinds.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited potential normalization in certification services growth after a strong Q4 2024. Tariffs and FX headwinds could also impact revenue and margins.

  • Analysts raised questions about sustained industrial growth, particularly amid elevated comparisons and evolving regulatory environments.

Final Takeaway

UL Solutions closed 2024 with strong financial performance and strategic progress, driven by megatrends like energy transition and digitalization. With mid-single-digit organic growth and a 24% adjusted EBITDA margin target for 2025, the company remains focused on leveraging its global reach, expanding capacity, and aligning investments with long-term growth opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals announces 1-for-5 reverse stock split; shares fall
data.description:
• 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:SXTP) on Thursday said it will effect a 1-for-5 reverse stock split of its common stock, par value $0.0001 per share.
  • The common stock will continue to trade on Nasdaq under the existing symbol “SXTP” and will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis when the market opens on February 24, 2025.
  • The company said the reverse stock split is primarily intended to bring the company into compliance with Nasdaq's $1.00 minimum bid price requirement for maintaining its Nasdaq listing.
  • SXTP is -17.32% to $0.4384.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hasbro's Q4 results buoys shares, launches PLAY DOH/Barbie line
data.description:
Buoyed by sales of video games and action figures, Hasbro’s (NASDAQ:HAS) less-than-expected decline in overall holiday sales helped bolster the company’s bottom-line above what Wall Street expected, driving shares higher on Thursday despite a lackluster outlook for FY25.

Excluding the impact from the divestiture of its eOne business, Hasbro’s (NASDAQ:HAS) Q4 revenue was down 3%. But thanks to a significant reduction in cost of sales and royalties, Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS) earned a profit of $0.46 per share, up from $0.38 a year ago and 11 cents better than expected. Operating margin expanded to 10.2% from a negative 3.9% last year.

By portfolio, net revenue from Hasbro’s (HAS) franchise brands like Dungeons and Dragons, fell 7%, and declined 11% for its portfolio brands (Monopoly, PLAY DOH, Nerf, etc), all of which was partially offset by an 18% jump in revenue for its licensed merchandise with Marvel, Star Wars, Disney, and Sesame Street.

Looking ahead to 2025, the company issued conservative guidance that includes a slight increase in revenue, operating margin of 21% to 22%, and adjusted EBITDA of $1.1B to $1.5B versus $1.06B in 2024.

As part of its longer-term goals, Hasbro (HAS) unveiled its “Playing to Win” strategy through “play-fueled engagement and partner-scaled co-investment.” As part of this strategy, Hasbro (HAS) issued medium-term guidance to cover 2025 to 2027. During this period, the company expects revenue to increase by mid-single-digits, debt to adjusted EBITDA of 2.5x, and adjusted operating margin to expand 50-100 basis points. By 2027, the company is expected to deliver $1B of gross cost savings, with approximately half contributing to the bottom line.

Finally, the company also announced a partnership with Mattel (MAT) to launch a PLAY-DOH Barbie collection. The collection will be sold by Hasbro (HAS) at Target (TGT) and on Hasbro’s (HAS) online store, available to most retailers by August 2025.

Shares were launched higher at the open, setting a 4-month-high.

data.title: Youdao targets AI-driven growth and moderate revenue increase in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Youdao, Inc. (NYSE:DAO) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Feng Zhou highlighted that Q4 2024 profitability improved significantly, with operating income reaching RMB84.2 million, a 10.3% year-over-year increase. The company achieved its first-ever full-year operating profit with income from operations of RMB148.8 million for 2024.
  • Net revenues for Q4 totaled RMB1.3 billion, a 9.5% year-over-year decline, primarily impacted by a 21.2% drop in learning services revenue. However, Zhou stated a strategic focus on high-demand courses like Youdao Lingshi to enhance operational health and sustainability.
  • Revenue from smart devices increased 8.1% year-over-year, driven by strong sales of the Youdao Dictionary Pen. Cumulative sales of these pens surpassed 10 million units to date.
  • CFO Wayne Li noted, "The net income attributable to ordinary shareholders for 2024 was over RMB 80 million, compared with a loss of around RMB 550 million in 2023." Li also emphasized plans for further cost optimizations and leveraging AI advancements to improve profitability in 2025.
  • Lei Jin, President, highlighted international expansion, including a partnership with Google to enhance overseas advertising capabilities and continued growth in key opinion leader (KOL) marketing.

Outlook

  • Feng Zhou outlined a dual focus on AI-driven education technology and online marketing technologies for 2025. He stressed the integration of reasoning models and automation into core offerings, including the launch of AI tutoring and college admissions advisory features.
  • Management remains committed to balancing "moderate revenue growth" with profitability amid ongoing economic pressures. Investments will prioritize AI-driven upgrades across products and services.
  • Specific initiatives include enhancing smart devices with AI features, such as the new Youdao SpaceOne pen, and expanding AI subscription services.
  • Li stated, "We anticipate continued moderate growth considering today's challenging economic environment, while keeping profitability a key focus."

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 net revenues were RMB1.3 billion, down 9.5% year-over-year. Learning services reported RMB617.7 million in net revenues, a 21.2% decline, while smart devices revenue increased to RMB240.4 million.
  • Total gross profit for Q4 was RMB640.8 million, with gross margin improvements in smart devices and online marketing services, reaching 43.9% and 34.2%, respectively.
  • Full-year 2024 revenue increased by 4.4% to RMB5.6 billion, and operating cash outflow improved significantly, narrowing to RMB67.9 million from RMB440 million in 2023.
  • Non-GAAP net income for Q4 was RMB91.8 million, compared to RMB69.3 million in Q4 2023.

Q&A

  • Brian Gong, Citi: "Can management elaborate on the outlook for 2025?" Zhou responded that the company will focus on AI integration, including reasoning models and AI tutoring, while maintaining financial discipline to balance growth and profitability.
  • Brenda Zhao, CICC: "What opportunities will arise for the online marketing business in 2025?" Lei Jin detailed plans to deepen partnerships with NetEase, expand international advertising, and leverage AI-driven optimizations for campaign efficiency.
  • Thomas Chong, Jefferies: "What is the priority for Learning Services this year?" Peng Su emphasized the importance of Youdao Lingshi, supported by AI features like learning diagnostics and video updates based on data-driven insights.
  • Bo Zhang, Huatai Securities: "What impact can we expect from integrating Confucius and DeepSeek models?" Zhou highlighted benefits such as enhanced AI tutoring, improved ad campaign optimizers, and rapid development of vertical AI models tailored for specific tasks.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism about growth prospects, focusing on AI-driven initiatives and international expansion. Their tone was neutral to slightly positive, with a focus on mitigating risks and assessing execution.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly in discussing AI advancements and financial improvements. Zhou emphasized, "AI tutoring will provide an enhanced learning experience," signaling optimism in product innovation.
  • Compared to Q3, management's tone remained consistent, focusing on disciplined growth and profitability, while analysts continued to press for clarity on strategic priorities and market opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue declined in Q4 compared to Q3, attributed to seasonal factors and a strategic shift toward high-demand learning services. This contrasts with Q3's 2.2% year-over-year revenue growth.
  • Guidance language emphasized moderate growth in 2025, aligning with Q3's focus on operational profitability and AI adoption.
  • Analysts in both quarters highlighted concerns over revenue trends and sought clarity on growth strategies, particularly in learning services and online marketing.
  • Management confidence remained steady, with continued emphasis on AI-driven innovation and financial discipline.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges include declining revenues in learning services and potential competitive pressures in the education and advertising sectors.
  • Management plans to mitigate risks by focusing on high-demand products, leveraging AI for personalized learning, and expanding international advertising partnerships.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the impact of economic conditions on growth and profitability, particularly in online marketing and smart devices.

Final Takeaway

Youdao's Q4 2024 earnings call highlighted significant progress in profitability and AI integration across its business lines. The company emphasized a strategic focus on leveraging AI-driven technologies to enhance learning services, expand online marketing capabilities, and drive innovation in smart devices. While revenue challenges persist, management remains optimistic about achieving moderate growth and improved financial performance in 2025, supported by new AI initiatives and international expansion.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Dow, S&P, and Nasdaq fall as investors digest Walmart's earnings report
data.description:
Wall Street averages slipped on Thursday as investors were left unimpressed with Walmart's (WMT) earnings report, a day after the S&P 500 notched another record high.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.6%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.7%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.9%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.51%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was lower by 2 basis points to 4.25%.

On the earnings front, Walmart (WMT) shares were down 5.7% as its profit, and sales outlook missed estimates amid stiff competition.

Palantir (PLTR) was also down 11.3% as Hegseth memo called for a sharp spending cut.

On the economic side, the initial jobless claims rose more than consensus in past week, while continuing claims came in line.

The Philly Fed manufacturing index weakened more than expected M/M in February.

Moreover, the leading economic index slipped 0.3% to 101.5 in January, weaker than the 0.0% consensus level that was forecasted.

Wall Street's major averages seesawed on Wednesday, but the benchmark S&P 500 eventually closed at a record high as investors digested the Federal Reserve's latest minutes and President Donald Trump's latest tariff action.

"The minutes of the January Federal Reserve meeting gave a cautious policy outlook. Nervousness about the economic consequences of Trump’s policies was clear. In particular, the potential for Trump to ignite a further wave of inflation was cited," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"Markets put in a divergent performance over the last 24 hours, with U.S. assets continuing to reach fresh highs, even as the rest of the world struggled on the back of President Donald Trump’s tariff threats and concerns about Ukraine," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

data.title: Sen. Warren wants DOJ to review Disney's deal with Fubo - report
data.description:
Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass) wants the Dept. of Justice to carefully review Disney's (NYSE:DIS) planned deal to combine its Hulu + Live with FuboTV (NYSE:FUBO) due to potential antitrust concerns. FUBO fell 2.5%.

Warren says the combination may lead to higher prices, according to a Variety report on Thursday, which cited a letter to the DOJ's antitrust division.

“This proposed acquisition raises significant concerns under antitrust law, would give Disney increased market power and incentives to increase costs for viewers, and should be regarded as another data point in Disney’s history of anticompetitive behavior,” Warren wrote.

FuboTV dropped 5% on Wednesday amid a report said that the DOJ is looking at the FUBO/Disney deal. DOJ attorneys are questioning media players if the combination may lead to Disney (DIS)

raising programming costs for pay-TV distributors.

data.title: Lam Research upgraded to Positive by Susquehanna following Investor Day
data.description:
Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) was upgraded to Positive from Neutral by the analysts at Susquehanna following its Investor Day in New York City on Wednesday, when the semiconductor company revealed new products and outlined a path to expand its served available market.

Shares were up about 3% during Thursday morning market action.

"We like the SAM expansion story laid out at the Analyst Day; the SAM expansion is also timed at further diversifying Lam's revenue mix by device type; with Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) remaining a top 3 customer, any turnaround in Samsung memory business should help Lam outgrow WFE; the NAND part of Lam's story is all about upgrade as the industry moves to >200k; operating framework continues to scale, thus driving OM expansion; and the 30% FCF margin target is exciting, with majority returned to shareholders," said Susquehanna analyst Mehdi Hosseini, in an investor note.

The global trading firm increased its price target on Lam to $125 from $75.

Lam also "introduced a CY2028 EPS target of $6-$7, while focusing on FCF margin of 30%, the majority of which will be returned to investors," Hosseini added. "This is in addition to the existing buyback authorization of $9.2B."

In addition to the guidance, Lam unveiled the ALTUS Halo and Akara.

Lam said ALTUS Halo is the world's first atomic layer deposition, or ALD, tool that harnesses the capabilities of the metal molybdenum in the production of leading-edge semiconductors. Akara delivers novel plasma processing technologies that enable etch precision and performance needed for 3D chip making, Lam noted.
